revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=天神社| 画像=[[画像:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|240px]]| 所在地=大阪市福島区玉川1丁目4-5| 祭神=[[菅原道真]], [[少彦名命]]| 社格=| 創建=伝 不詳| 本殿=| 別名=福島 下の天神| 例祭=| 位置=[[画像:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]| }}
'''天神社'''（てんじんしゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[福島区]]玉川1丁目にある[[神社]]である。また、俗に''福島 下の天神''といわれることもある。
==由緒== 社伝によれば、[[901年]]に[[菅原道真]]が、[[藤原時平]]により九州[[太宰府]]へ配転させられた際、当社に参拝し海路の平穏を祈ったと伝えられている。また[[菅原道真]]は、この辺りを餓飢島と呼ばれていたのを福島と名づけられた。以降、当社は人々に天神様として尊崇を集められたことが起源とされている。
==祭神== [[画像:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] {{Commonscat|Tenjinsya (Fukushima-ku) shrine, Osaka}} 主祭神 * [[少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） * [[菅原道真]]（すがはら みちざね）
==社史== * 創建年 不詳 * [[元禄時代]] 現在の拝殿が建てられたと伝えられている * [[1872年]] (明治5年) 村社に列せられる * [[1907年]] (明治40年) 豊光神社を合祀
==境内社== * 豊光神社 * 天照皇大神宮外六坐社 * 宮比事平神社 * [[厳島神社]] (榎社)
==福島三天神== * 上の天神 ： [[福島区]] 福島 2丁目8-1 ::現在の[[福島天満宮]]。[[菅原道真]]を祀っている。 * 中の天神 ： [[福島区]] 福島 4丁目1（下福島公園横） ::[[第二次世界大戦]]の空襲で消失したため、[[福島天満宮]]に合祀された。 * 下の天神 ： [[福島区]] 玉川 1丁目4-5 ::当社。
==祭事==
==交通機関== 鉄道 * [[京阪中之島線]] [[中之島駅]] 徒歩3分 （2008年秋開業予定） * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[JR東西線|東西線]] [[新福島駅]] 徒歩10分 * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[大阪環状線]]、[[阪神本線]] [[福島駅 (大阪府)|福島駅]] 徒歩15分 * [[大阪市営地下鉄]] [[大阪市営地下鉄千日前線|千日前線]] [[玉川駅 (大阪府)|玉川駅]] 徒歩7分
バス * [[大阪市営バス]] 55系統 堂島大橋北詰 徒歩2分 * [[大阪市営バス]] 53系統 堂島大橋 徒歩3分 * [[大阪市営バス]] 赤バス福島ループ 玉川1丁目停留所下車 徒歩1分
==外部リンク== * [http://www.city.osaka.jp/fukushima/spot01.html 福島区役所：名所旧跡]
{{DEFAULTSORT:てんじんしゃ}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:福島区]]

{{神社| 名称=天神社| 画像=[[画像:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|240px]]| 所在地=大阪市福島区玉川1丁目4-5| 祭神=[[菅原道真]], [[少彦名命]]| 社格=| 創建=伝 不詳| 本殿=| 別名=福島 下の天神| 例祭=| 位置=[[画像:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]| }}

==祭神== [[画像:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] {{Commonscat|Tenjinsya (Fukushima-ku) shrine, Osaka}} 主祭神 * [[少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） * [[菅原道真]]（すがはら みちざね）

バス * [[大阪市営バス]] 55系統 堂島大橋北詰 徒歩2分 * [[大阪市営バス]] 53系統 堂島大橋 徒歩3分 * [[大阪市営バス]] 赤バス福島ループ 玉川1丁目停留所下車 徒歩1分
==外部リンク== * [http://www.city.osaka.jp/fukushima/spot01.html 福島区役所：名所旧跡]

{{RFD notice|'''対象リダイレクト:'''[[Wikipedia:リダイレクトの削除依頼/2011年5月#RFD天神社 (福島区)|天神社 (福島区)（2011年5月依頼）]]}} {{神社| 名称=天神社| 画像=[[画像:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|240px]]| 所在地=大阪市福島区玉川1丁目4-5| 祭神=[[菅原道真]], [[少彦名命]]| 社格=村社| 創建=伝 不詳| 本殿=| 別名=福島 下の天神| 例祭=| 位置=[[画像:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]| }}
'''天神社'''（てんじんしゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[福島区]]玉川1丁目にある[[神社]]である。また、俗に''福島 下の天神''といわれることもある。
==由緒== 社伝によれば、[[901年]]に[[菅原道真]]が、[[藤原時平]]により九州[[大宰府]]へ配転させられた際、当社に参拝し海路の平穏を祈ったと伝えられている。また菅原道真は、この辺りを餓飢島と呼ばれていたのを福島と名づけられた。以降、当社は人々に天神様として尊崇を集められたことが起源とされている。
==祭神== [[画像:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] {{Commonscat|Tenjinsya (Fukushima-ku) shrine, Osaka}} 主祭神 * [[少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） * [[菅原道真]]（すがはら みちざね）
==社史== * 創建年 不詳 * [[元禄時代]] 現在の拝殿が建てられたと伝えられている * [[1872年]] (明治5年) 村社に列せられる * [[1907年]] (明治40年) 豊光神社を合祀
==境内社== * 豊光神社 * 天照皇大神宮外六坐社 * 宮比事平神社 * [[厳島神社]] (榎社)
==福島三天神== * 上の天神 ： 福島区 福島 2丁目8-1 ::現在の[[福島天満宮]]。菅原道真を祀っている。 * 中の天神 ： 福島区 福島 4丁目1（下福島公園横） ::[[第二次世界大戦]]の空襲で消失したため、[[福島天満宮]]に合祀された。 * 下の天神 ： 福島区 玉川 1丁目4-5 ::当社。
==祭事==
==交通機関== 鉄道 * [[京阪中之島線]] [[中之島駅]] 徒歩3分 * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[JR東西線|東西線]] [[新福島駅]] 徒歩10分 * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[大阪環状線]] [[福島駅 (JR西日本)|福島駅]] 徒歩10分 * [[阪神本線]] [[福島駅 (阪神)|福島駅]] 徒歩15分 * [[大阪市営地下鉄]] [[大阪市営地下鉄千日前線|千日前線]] [[玉川駅 (大阪府)|玉川駅]] 徒歩7分
バス * [[大阪市営バス]] 55系統 堂島大橋北詰 徒歩2分 * [[大阪市営バス]] 53系統 堂島大橋 徒歩3分 * [[大阪市営バス]] 赤バス福島ループ 玉川1丁目停留所下車 徒歩1分
==外部リンク== * [http://www.city.osaka.jp/fukushima/spot01.html 福島区役所：名所旧跡]
{{coor title d|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|scale:2500}}

'''天神社'''（てんじんしゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[福島区]]玉川1丁目にある[[神社]]である。また、俗に''福島 下の天神''といわれることもある。
==由緒== 社伝によれば、[[901年]]に[[菅原道真]]が、[[藤原時平]]により九州[[大宰府]]へ配転させられた際、当社に参拝し海路の平穏を祈ったと伝えられている。また菅原道真は、この辺りを餓飢島と呼ばれていたのを福島と名づけられた。以降、当社は人々に天神様として尊崇を集められたことが起源とされている。
==祭神== [[画像:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] {{Commonscat|Tenjinsha (Fukushima-ku, Osaka)}} 主祭神 * [[少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） * [[菅原道真]]（すがはら みちざね）
==社史== * 創建年 不詳 * [[元禄時代]] 現在の拝殿が建てられたと伝えられている * [[1872年]] (明治5年) 村社に列せられる * [[1907年]] (明治40年) 豊光神社を合祀
==境内社== * 豊光神社 * 天照皇大神宮外六坐社 * 宮比事平神社 * [[厳島神社]] (榎社)
==福島三天神== * 上の天神 ： 福島区 福島 2丁目8-1 ::現在の[[福島天満宮]]。菅原道真を祀っている。 * 中の天神 ： 福島区 福島 4丁目1（下福島公園横） ::[[第二次世界大戦]]の空襲で消失したため、[[福島天満宮]]に合祀された。 * 下の天神 ： 福島区 玉川 1丁目4-5 ::当社。
==祭事==
==交通機関== 鉄道 * [[京阪中之島線]] [[中之島駅]] 徒歩3分 * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[JR東西線|東西線]] [[新福島駅]] 徒歩10分 * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[大阪環状線]] [[福島駅 (JR西日本)|福島駅]] 徒歩10分 * [[阪神本線]] [[福島駅 (阪神)|福島駅]] 徒歩15分 * [[大阪市営地下鉄]] [[大阪市営地下鉄千日前線|千日前線]] [[玉川駅 (大阪府)|玉川駅]] 徒歩7分
バス * [[大阪市営バス]] 55系統 堂島大橋北詰 徒歩2分 * [[大阪市営バス]] 53系統 堂島大橋 徒歩3分 * [[大阪市営バス]] 赤バス福島ループ 玉川1丁目停留所下車 徒歩1分

{{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしや おおさかしふくしまく}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:福島区の歴史]] [[Category:福島区の建築物]]

{{神社| 名称=天神社| 画像=[[画像:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]]| 所在地=大阪市福島区玉川1丁目4-5| 祭神=[[菅原道真]], [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]| 社格=村社| 創建=伝 不詳| 本殿=| 別名=福島 下の天神| 例祭=| 位置=[[画像:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]| }} '''天神社'''（てんじんしゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[福島区]]玉川にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[村社]]。また、俗に'''福島 下の天神'''といわれることもある。
==祭神== [[画像:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ;主祭神 * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） * [[菅原道真]]（すがはら みちざね）
==由緒== 社伝によれば、[[901年]]に[[菅原道真]]が、[[藤原時平]]により九州[[大宰府]]へ配転させられた際、当社に参拝し海路の平穏を祈ったと伝えられている。また、一説では天満天神の夏祭りでの「船渡御」での鉾流し神事において、堂島川のその鉾の流れ着いたところに行宮の位置を決めていた際に、流れ着いた時の行宮跡とする説がある<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html 大阪市福島区：名所・旧跡 （…>みどころ>みどころガイド）]</ref>。また菅原道真はこの辺りを餓飢島と呼ばれていたのを福島と名づけられた。以降、当社は人々に[[天神信仰|天神様]]として尊崇を集められたことが起源とされている。
[[元禄時代]]に現在の拝殿が建てられたと伝えられている。[[1872年]] (明治5年) 村社に列せられる。[[1907年]] (明治40年)に 豊光神社を合祀する。
==境末社== * 豊光神社 * 天照皇大神宮外六坐社 * 宮比事平神社 * 厳島神社 (榎社)
==福島三天神== * 上の天神 ： 福島区 福島 2丁目8-1 ::現在の[[福島天満宮]]。菅原道真を祀っている。 * 中の天神 ： 福島区 福島 4丁目1（下福島公園横） ::[[第二次世界大戦]]の空襲で消失したため、福島天満宮に合祀された。 * 下の天神 ： 福島区 玉川 1丁目4-5 ::当社。

* 最寄バス停：[[大阪市営バス]] [[大阪市営バス鶴町営業所#55号系統|55系統]] 堂島大橋北詰 徒歩2分 * 最寄バス停：大阪市営バス [[大阪市営バス中津営業所#53号系統|53系統]] 堂島大橋 徒歩3分

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=天神社| 画像=[[画像:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]]| 所在地=大阪市福島区玉川1丁目4-5| 祭神=[[菅原道真]], [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]| 社格=村社| 創建=伝 不詳| 本殿=| 別名=福島 下の天神| 例祭=| 位置=[[画像:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]| }} '''天神社'''（てんじんしゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[福島区]]玉川にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[村社]]。また、俗に'''福島 下の天神'''といわれることもある。
==祭神== [[画像:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ;主祭神 * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） * [[菅原道真]]（すがはら みちざね）
==由緒== 社伝によれば、[[901年]]に[[菅原道真]]が、[[藤原時平]]により九州[[大宰府]]へ配転させられた際、当社に参拝し海路の平穏を祈ったと伝えられている。また、一説では天満天神の夏祭りでの「船渡御」での鉾流し神事において、堂島川のその鉾の流れ着いたところに行宮の位置を決めていた際に、流れ着いた時の行宮跡とする説がある<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html 大阪市福島区：名所・旧跡 （…>みどころ>みどころガイド）]</ref>。また菅原道真はこの辺りを餓飢島と呼ばれていたのを福島と名づけられた。以降、当社は人々に[[天神信仰|天神様]]として尊崇を集められたことが起源とされている。
[[元禄時代]]に現在の拝殿が建てられたと伝えられている。[[1872年]] (明治5年) 村社に列せられる。[[1907年]] (明治40年)に 豊光神社を合祀する。
==境末社== * 豊光神社 * 天照皇大神宮外六坐社 * 宮比事平神社 * 厳島神社 (榎社)
==福島三天神== * 上の天神 ： 福島区 福島 2丁目8-1 ::現在の[[福島天満宮]]。菅原道真を祀っている。 * 中の天神 ： 福島区 福島 4丁目1（下福島公園横） ::[[第二次世界大戦]]の空襲で消失したため、福島天満宮に合祀された。 * 下の天神 ： 福島区 玉川 1丁目4-5 ::当社。
== 現地情報 == ;所在地 *大阪市福島区玉川1丁目4-5
;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[京阪電気鉄道|京阪]][[京阪中之島線|中之島線]] [[中之島駅]] 徒歩3分 [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[JR東西線|東西線]] [[新福島駅]] 徒歩10分 [[大阪環状線]] [[福島駅 (JR西日本)|福島駅]] 徒歩10分 [[阪神電気鉄道|阪神]][[阪神本線|本線]] [[福島駅 (阪神)|福島駅]] 徒歩15分 [[Osaka Metro千日前線|千日前線]] [[玉川駅 (大阪府)|玉川駅]] 徒歩7分
* 最寄バス停：[[大阪シティバス]] [[大阪シティバス鶴町営業所#55号系統|55系統]] 堂島大橋北詰 徒歩2分 [[大阪シティバス中津営業所#53号系統|53系統]] 堂島大橋 徒歩3分
== 脚注 == <!--=== 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} -->
=== 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Tenjinsha (Fukushima-ku, Osaka)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai5sibu/fukusima-ku/m01c_04_fukusima_ten.html 天神社 - 大阪府神社庁]
{{神道 横}} {{coor title d|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|scale:2500}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしや おおさかしふくしまく}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:福島区の歴史]] [[Category:福島区の建築物]]

== Tohoku Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tome)|ja|天神社 (登米市)}} - Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}, Miyagi Prefecture.
== Kanto Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Shiroi)|ja|天神社 (白井市)}} - Located in {{ill|Shiroi, Chiba|ja|白井市}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Isumi) - Located in Isumi, Chiba Prefecture, in Misaki Town, Choja. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nishitokyo)|ja|天神社 (西東京市)}} - Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo|ja|西東京市}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Mitaka)|ja|天神社 (三鷹市)}} - Located in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Shiki)|ja|天神社 (志木市)}} - Located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kamisato)|ja|天神社 (上里町五明)}} - Located in Kamisato, Saitama. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Saitama-Chuo)|ja|天神社 (さいたま市中央区)}} - Located in Saitama City, Chuo Ward. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Saitama-Minamiku-Futonoya)|ja|天神社 (さいたま市見沼区風渡野)}} - Located in Futonoya, Minamiku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Saitama-Midoriku-Terayama)|ja|天神社 (さいたま市緑区寺山)}} - Located in Terayama, Midoriku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yashio-Ozone)|ja|天神社 (八潮市大曽根)}} - Located in Ozone, Yashio City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kuki-Kitahiroshima)|ja|天神社 (久喜市北広島)}} - Located in Kitahiroshima, Kuki City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Hanyu-Kamikawasaki)|ja|天神社 (羽生市上川崎)}} - Located in Kamikawasaki, Hanyu City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)|ja|天神社 (加須市平永)}} - Located in Hiranaga, Kazo City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yokosuka)|ja|天神社 (横須賀市)}} ({{ill|Kurihama Tenjinja|ja|久里浜天神社}}) - Located in {{ill|Yokosuka|ja|横須賀市}}, Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kurobe)|ja|天神社 (黒部市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kurobe, Toyama|ja|黒部市}}, Toyama Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Fujikawaguchiko-Oarashi)|ja|天神社 (富士河口湖町大嵐)}} - Located in Oarashi, {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko|ja|富士河口湖町}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Oarashi Tenjinja|ja|大嵐天神社}} * {{ill|Tenmantenjinja (Iida)|ja|天満天神社 (飯田市)}} - Located in Nakanomachi, {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, Nagano Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Fumon-in (Iida)|ja|普門院 (飯田市)}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nagoya)|ja|天神社 (名古屋市中区)}} - Located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Nagoya|ja|中区 (名古屋市)}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Sakura Tenjinja|ja|桜天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Obu)|ja|天神社 (大府市)}} - Located in Nagasago, {{ill|Obu, Aichi|ja|大府市}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Nagasago Tenjinja|ja|長草天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Toyokawa)|ja|天神社 (豊川市)}} - Located in Mikamicho, {{ill|Toyokawa, Aichi|ja|豊川市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Komaki)|ja|天神社 (小牧市)}} - Located in Shimazu, {{ill|Komaki|ja|小牧市}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ Tenmantenjinja * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kasukawa)|ja|天神社 (春日井市勝川町)}} - Located in Katsukawacho, {{ill|Kasugai|ja|春日井市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kiyosu)|ja|天神社 (清須市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kiyosu|ja|清須市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Mihama)|ja|天神社 (愛知県美浜町)}} - Located in {{ill|Mihama, Aichi|ja|美浜町 (愛知県)}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Kowa Tenjinja|ja|河和天神社}} * {{ill|Kitano Tenjinja (Konan)|ja|北野天神社 (江南市)}} - Located in Kitanocho, {{ill|Konan, Aichi|ja|江南市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Hokai Tenjinja|ja|宝海天神社}} - Located in Homi Town, {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yoshida Tenjinja|ja|吉田天神社}} - Located in Hirokoji, {{ill|Toyohashi, Aichi|ja|豊橋市}}, Aichi Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Osaka-Fukushima)|ja|天神社 (大阪市福島区)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|ja|福島区}}, Osaka Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Osaka Kita-ku) - Located in Kokubunji, Kita-ku, Osaka. ⇒ {{ill|Yodogawa Tenjinja|ja|淀川天神社}} * Tenjinja (Osaka Higashisumiyoshi-ku) - Located in Kuwazu, Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka. ⇒ {{ill|Kuwazu Tenjinja|ja|桑津天神社}} * Tenjinja (Toyonaka) - Located in Kamishinden, {{ill|Toyonaka|ja|豊中市}}, Osaka Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Kamishinden Tenjinja|ja|上新田天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kawachinagano)|ja|天神社 (河内長野市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kadoma)|ja|天神社 (門真市)}} - Located in Tsukidecho, {{ill|Kadoma|ja|門真市}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kizugawa)|ja|天神社 (木津川市)}} - Located in Yamashiro, {{ill|Kizugawa|ja|木津川市}}, Kyoto Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nara-Takabatake)|ja|天神社 (奈良市高畑町)}} - Located in Takabatakecho, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yamatotakada)|ja|天神社 (大和高田市)}} - Located in Miwacho, {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Sakurai) - Located in Saimiyama, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture. * Tenjinja - Located in Kawahara 4-chome, Kusatsu, Shiga Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Akashi)|ja|天神社 (明石市)}} - Located in {{ill|Akashi|ja|明石市}}, Hyogo Prefecture.
== Shikoku Region == * {{ill|Tokushima Bizen Tenjinja|ja|徳島眉山天神社}} - Located in {{ill|Bizencho|ja|眉山町}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima)|ja|天神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in Kitamiya, Tokushima. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima-Iritacho)|ja|天神社 (徳島市入田町)}} - Located in Irita, Tokushima.

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | Image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | Location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | Image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | Location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | Image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | Location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)
## **References** <!--=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} -->
### **Sources** {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
## **External Links** {{Commonscat|Tenjinsha (Fukushima-ku, Osaka)}} - [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai5sibu/fukusima-ku/m01c_04_fukusima_ten.html Tenjin Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] (in Japanese)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | Image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | Location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.

revision.comment: fixing the broken shrine infoboxes again
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)
## **References** <!--=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} -->
### **Sources** {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
## **External Links** {{Commonscat|Tenjinsha (Fukushima-ku, Osaka)}} - [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai5sibu/fukusima-ku/m01c_04_fukusima_ten.html Tenjin Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Association] (in Japanese)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)
## **References** <!--=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}} -->

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ### Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** ### **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
### **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] === Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** === **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
=== **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
## **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] === Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
## **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
## **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
## **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
## **Access** === **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
=== **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
**Tenjin Shrine** (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as **"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"** (福島 下の天神).
== **Enshrined Deities** [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] === Main Deities - **[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]** (少彦名命) - **[[Sugawara no Michizane]]** (菅原道真)
== **History** According to shrine tradition, in **901**, [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to **[[Dazaifu]]** by **[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]**. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a **divine float** (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the **Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival** drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary **gyōkū** (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from **Gakishima** (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to **Fukushima** (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a **Tenjin Shrine** (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the **Genroku period** (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In **1872** (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In **1907** (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with **Toyomitsu Shrine**.
== **Sub-Shrines** - **Toyomitsu Shrine** - **Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines** - **Miyabito Heisha Shrine** - **Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)**
== **Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines** - **Upper Tenjin** (*Kami no Tenjin*) – **Fukushima Tenmangū**, located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - **Middle Tenjin** (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in **World War II** air raids. - **Lower Tenjin** (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – **This shrine**.
== **Access** === **Address** - 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
=== **Transportation** - **Nearest Train Stations**: - **Keihan Electric Railway** – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] **Nakanoshima Station** (3-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[JR Tōzai Line]] **Shin-Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **JR West** – [[Osaka Loop Line]] **Fukushima Station** (10-minute walk) - **Hanshin Electric Railway** – [[Hanshin Main Line]] **Fukushima Station** (15-minute walk) - **Osaka Metro Chūō Line** – **Tamagawa Station** (7-minute walk)
- **Nearest Bus Stops**: - **Osaka City Bus** **55 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop** (2-minute walk) - **Osaka City Bus** **53 Route** – **Dōjima Ōhashi Stop** (3-minute walk)
