{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).
== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==
[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]
=== Main Deities ===
- '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) - '''[[Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (菅原道真)
== '''History''' ==
According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.
== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==
- '''Toyomitsu Shrine''' - '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines''' - '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine''' - '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''
== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==
- '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids. - '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.
== '''Access''' ==
== '''Address''' ==
- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

- '''Nearest Train Stations''': - '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk) - '''JR West''' – [[JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk) - '''JR West''' – [[Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk) - '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk) - '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)
- '''Nearest Bus Stops''': - '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk) - '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).
== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==
[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]
=== Main Deities ===
- '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) - '''[[Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (菅原道真)
== '''History''' ==
According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.
== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==
- '''Toyomitsu Shrine''' - '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines''' - '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine''' - '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''
== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==
- '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids. - '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.
== '''Access''' ==
== '''Address''' ==
- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
=== '''Transportation''' ===
- '''Nearest Train Stations''': - '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk) - '''JR West''' – [[JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk) - '''JR West''' – [[Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk) - '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk) - '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)
- '''Nearest Bus Stops''': - '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk) - '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).
== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==
[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]
=== Main Deities ===
- '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) - '''[[Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (菅原道真)
== '''History''' ==
According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.
== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==
- '''Toyomitsu Shrine''' - '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines''' - '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine''' - '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''
== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==
- '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. - '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids. - '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.
== '''Access''' ==
== '''Address''' ==
- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
=== '''Transportation''' ===
- '''Nearest Train Stations''': - '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk) - '''JR West''' – [[JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk) - '''JR West''' – [[Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk) - '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk) - '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)
- '''Nearest Bus Stops''': - '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk) - '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).
== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==
[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]
=== Main Deities ===
* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) * '''[[Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (菅原道真)
== '''History''' ==
According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.
== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==
* '''Toyomitsu Shrine''' * '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines''' * '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine''' * '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''
== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==
* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. * '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids. * '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.
== '''Access''' ==
== '''Address''' ==
- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
=== '''Transportation''' ===
* '''Nearest Train Stations''': * '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk) * '''JR West''' – [[JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk) * '''JR West''' – [[Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk) * '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk) * '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)
* '''Nearest Bus Stops''': * '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk) * '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).
== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==
[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]
=== Main Deities ===
* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) * '''[[Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (菅原道真)
== '''History''' ==
According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.
== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==
* '''Toyomitsu Shrine''' * '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines''' * '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine''' * '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''
== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==
* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. * '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids. * '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.
== '''Access''' ==
== '''Address''' ==
- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "菅原道真" to "Sugawara no Michizane"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).
== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==
[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]
=== Main Deities ===
* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) * '''[[Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)
== '''History''' ==
According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.
== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==
* '''Toyomitsu Shrine''' * '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines''' * '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine''' * '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''
== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==
* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. * '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids. * '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.
== '''Access''' ==
== '''Address''' ==
- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
=== '''Transportation''' ===
* '''Nearest Train Stations''': ** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk) ** '''JR West''' – [[JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk) ** '''JR West''' – [[Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk) ** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk) ** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)
* '''Nearest Bus Stops''': ** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk) ** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)
=== '''References''' ===

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).
== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==
[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]
=== Main Deities ===
* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) * '''[[Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)
== '''History''' ==
According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.
== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==
* '''Toyomitsu Shrine''' * '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines''' * '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine''' * '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''
== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==
* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. * '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids. * '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.
== '''Access''' ==
== '''Address''' ==
- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
=== '''Transportation''' ===
* '''Nearest Train Stations''': ** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk) ** '''JR West''' – [[JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk) ** '''JR West''' – [[Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk) ** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk) ** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)
* '''Nearest Bus Stops''': ** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk) ** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).
== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==
[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]
=== Main Deities ===
* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) * '''[[Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)
== '''History''' ==
According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.
== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==
* '''Toyomitsu Shrine''' * '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines''' * '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine''' * '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''
== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==
* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. * '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids. * '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.
== '''Access''' ==
== '''Address''' ==
- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjin Shrine | image = [[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[Osaka City]] | Deities = [[Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*) | Established = Unknown (legendary) | Main Hall = | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin" | Festival = | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).
== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==
[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]
=== Main Deities ===
* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命) * '''[[Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)
== '''History''' ==
According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.
Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).
During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.
== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==
* '''Toyomitsu Shrine''' * '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines''' * '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine''' * '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''
== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==
* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward. * '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids. * '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.
== '''Access''' ==
== '''Address''' ==
- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''': ** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk) ** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=天神社 | 画像=[[画像:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg|250px]] | 所在地=大阪市福島区玉川1丁目4-5 | 祭神=[[菅原道真]], [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] | 社格=村社 | 創建=伝 不詳 | 本殿= | 別名=福島 下の天神 | 例祭= | 位置=[[画像:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]] }}
'''天神社'''（てんじんしゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[福島区]]玉川にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[村社]]。また、俗に'''福島 下の天神'''といわれることもある。
==祭神== [[画像:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]] ;主祭神 * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]（すくなひこのみこと） * [[菅原道真]]（すがはら みちざね）
==由緒== 社伝によれば、[[901年]]に[[菅原道真]]が、[[藤原時平]]により九州[[大宰府]]へ配転させられた際、当社に参拝し海路の平穏を祈ったと伝えられている。また、一説では天満天神の夏祭りでの「船渡御」での鉾流し神事において、堂島川のその鉾の流れ着いたところに行宮の位置を決めていた際に、流れ着いた時の行宮跡とする説がある<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html 大阪市福島区：名所・旧跡 （…>みどころ>みどころガイド）]</ref>。また菅原道真はこの辺りを餓飢島と呼ばれていたのを福島と名づけられた。以降、当社は人々に[[天神信仰|天神様]]として尊崇を集められたことが起源とされている。
[[元禄時代]]に現在の拝殿が建てられたと伝えられている。[[1872年]] (明治5年) 村社に列せられる。[[1907年]] (明治40年)に 豊光神社を合祀する。
==境末社== * 豊光神社 * 天照皇大神宮外六坐社 * 宮比事平神社 * 厳島神社 (榎社)
==福島三天神== ; 上の天神 ： 福島区 福島 2丁目8-1 : 現在の[[福島天満宮]]。菅原道真を祀っている。 ; 中の天神 ： 福島区 福島 4丁目1（下福島公園横） : [[第二次世界大戦]]の空襲で消失したため、福島天満宮に合祀された。 ; 下の天神 ： 福島区 玉川 1丁目4-5 : 当社。
== 現地情報 == ;所在地 *大阪市福島区玉川1丁目4-5
;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[京阪電気鉄道|京阪]][[京阪中之島線|中之島線]] [[中之島駅]] 徒歩3分 [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]] [[JR東西線|東西線]] [[新福島駅]] 徒歩10分 [[大阪環状線]] [[福島駅 (JR西日本)|福島駅]] 徒歩10分 [[阪神電気鉄道|阪神]][[阪神本線|本線]] [[福島駅 (阪神)|福島駅]] 徒歩15分 [[Osaka Metro千日前線|千日前線]] [[玉川駅 (大阪府)|玉川駅]] 徒歩7分
* 最寄バス停：[[大阪シティバス]] [[大阪シティバス鶴町営業所#55号系統|55系統]] 堂島大橋北詰 徒歩2分 [[大阪シティバス中津営業所#53号系統|53系統]] 堂島大橋 徒歩3分
== 脚注 == <!--=== 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} -->
=== 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Tenjinsha (Fukushima-ku, Osaka)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai5sibu/fukusima-ku/m01c_04_fukusima_ten.html 天神社 - 大阪府神社庁]
{{神道 横}} {{coord|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|scale:2500|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしや おおさかしふくしまく}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:福島区の歴史]] [[Category:福島区の建築物]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tenjinsha (Fukushima-ku, Osaka)]][[ja:天神社 (大阪市福島区)]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11443281]]
Wikidata name: Tenjinsha
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
 | Name = Tenjin Shrine
 | image = File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg
 | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[:en:Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]]
 | Deities = [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
 | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*)
 | Established = Unknown (legendary)
 | Main Hall =
 | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin"
 | Festival =
 | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]
| location = {{ill|Tamagawa|qid=Q56349548}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qid=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
 }}

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

=== '''References''' ===

=== '''Sources''' ===

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]

 | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*)
 | Established = Unknown (legendary)
 | Main Hall =
 | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin"
 | Festival =
 | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]
| location = {{ill|Tamagawa|WD=Q56349548}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
 }}

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)
