'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

Wikidata name: Tenjinsha
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
 | Name = Tenjin Shrine
 | image = File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg
 | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[:en:Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]]
 | Deities = [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
 | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*)
 | Established = Unknown (legendary)
 | Main Hall =
 | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin"
 | Festival =
 | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]
| location = {{ill|Tamagawa|WD=Q56349548}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
 }}

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

Wikidata name: Tenjinsha
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
 | Name = Tenjin Shrine
 | image = File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg
 | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[:en:Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]]
 | Deities = [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
 | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*)
 | Established = Unknown (legendary)
 | Main Hall =
 | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin"
 | Festival =
 | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]
| location = {{ill|Tamagawa|WD=Q56349548}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
 }}

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]

 | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*)
 | Established = Unknown (legendary)
 | Main Hall =

| location = {{ill|Tamagawa|WD=Q56349548}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
 | Name = Tenjin Shrine
 | image = File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg
 | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[:en:Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]]
 | Deities = [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
 | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*)
 | Established = Unknown (legendary)
 | Main Hall =
 | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin"
 | Festival =
 | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]
| location = {{ill|Tamagawa|WD=Q56349548}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
 }}

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
 | Name = Tenjin Shrine
 | image = File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg
 | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, [[:en:Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Fukushima Ward]], [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]]
 | Deities = [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]], [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
 | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*)
 | Established = Unknown (legendary)
 | Main Hall =
 | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin"
 | Festival =
 | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]
| location = {{ill|Tamagawa|WD=Q56349548}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
 }}

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':

** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
 }}

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''[[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''[[:en:Dazaifu|Dazaifu]]''' by '''[[:en:Fujiwara no Tokihira|Fujiwara no Tokihira]]'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Keihan Nakanoshima Line|Keihan Nakanoshima Line]] '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:JR Tōzai Line|JR Tōzai Line]] '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – [[:en:Osaka Loop Line|Osaka Loop Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – [[:en:Hanshin Main Line|Hanshin Main Line]] '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''{{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu}}''' by '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|WD=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira}}'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|en|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|WD=Q126331|lt=Keihan Nakanoshima Line}} '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|JR Tōzai Line|en|JR Tōzai Line|WD=Q1072835|lt=JR Tōzai Line}} '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|Osaka Loop Line|en|Osaka Loop Line|WD=Q1142127|lt=Osaka Loop Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Hanshin Main Line|en|Hanshin Main Line|WD=Q1053647|lt=Hanshin Main Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
 | Name = Tenjin Shrine
 | image = File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg
 | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, {{ill|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|en|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985728|lt=Fukushima Ward}}, [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]]
 | Deities = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}, [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
 | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*)
 | Established = Unknown (legendary)
 | Main Hall =
 | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin"
 | Festival =
 | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]
| location = {{ill|Tamagawa|WD=Q56349548}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
 }}

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''{{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu}}''' by '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|WD=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira}}'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|en|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|WD=Q126331|lt=Keihan Nakanoshima Line}} '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|JR Tōzai Line|en|JR Tōzai Line|WD=Q1072835|lt=JR Tōzai Line}} '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|Osaka Loop Line|en|Osaka Loop Line|WD=Q1142127|lt=Osaka Loop Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Hanshin Main Line|en|Hanshin Main Line|WD=Q1053647|lt=Hanshin Main Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''{{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu}}''' by '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|WD=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira}}'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|en|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|WD=Q126331|lt=Keihan Nakanoshima Line}} '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|JR Tōzai Line|en|JR Tōzai Line|WD=Q1072835|lt=JR Tōzai Line}} '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|Osaka Loop Line|en|Osaka Loop Line|WD=Q1142127|lt=Osaka Loop Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Hanshin Main Line|en|Hanshin Main Line|WD=Q1053647|lt=Hanshin Main Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''{{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu}}''' by '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|WD=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira}}'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|en|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|WD=Q126331|lt=Keihan Nakanoshima Line}} '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|JR Tōzai Line|en|JR Tōzai Line|WD=Q1072835|lt=JR Tōzai Line}} '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|Osaka Loop Line|en|Osaka Loop Line|WD=Q1142127|lt=Osaka Loop Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Hanshin Main Line|en|Hanshin Main Line|WD=Q1053647|lt=Hanshin Main Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
 | Name = Tenjin Shrine
 | image = File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg
 | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, {{ill|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|en|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985728|lt=Fukushima Ward}}, [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]]
 | Deities = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}, [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
 | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*)
 | Established = Unknown (legendary)
 | Main Hall =
 | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin"
 | Festival =
 | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]
| location = {{ill|Tamagawa|WD=Q56349548}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
 }}

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''{{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu}}''' by '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|WD=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira}}'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|en|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|WD=Q126331|lt=Keihan Nakanoshima Line}} '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|JR Tōzai Line|en|JR Tōzai Line|WD=Q1072835|lt=JR Tōzai Line}} '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|Osaka Loop Line|en|Osaka Loop Line|WD=Q1142127|lt=Osaka Loop Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Hanshin Main Line|en|Hanshin Main Line|WD=Q1053647|lt=Hanshin Main Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
 | Name = Tenjin Shrine
 | image = File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg
 | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, {{ill|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|en|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985728|lt=Fukushima Ward}}, [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]]
 | Deities = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}, [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
 | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*)
 | Established = Unknown (legendary)
 | Main Hall =
 | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin"
 | Festival =
 | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]
| location = {{ill|Tamagawa|WD=Q56349548}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
 }}

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''{{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu}}''' by '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|WD=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira}}'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|en|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|WD=Q126331|lt=Keihan Nakanoshima Line}} '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|JR Tōzai Line|en|JR Tōzai Line|WD=Q1072835|lt=JR Tōzai Line}} '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|Osaka Loop Line|en|Osaka Loop Line|WD=Q1142127|lt=Osaka Loop Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Hanshin Main Line|en|Hanshin Main Line|WD=Q1053647|lt=Hanshin Main Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
 | Name = Tenjin Shrine
 | image = File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-02.jpg
 | location = 1-4-5 Tamagawa, {{ill|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|en|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|WD=Q985728|lt=Fukushima Ward|1=Fukushima-ku, Osaka}}, [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]]
 | Deities = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
 | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*)
 | Established = Unknown (legendary)
 | Main Hall =
 | Alias = Fukushima "Lower Tenjin"
 | Festival =
 | Map = [[File:Fukushima-Tenmangu-Map.svg|200px]]
| location = {{ill|Tamagawa|WD=Q56349548|1=Tamagawa}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.6885|N|135.4825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
 }}

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神社, *Tenjinsha*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Tamagawa, Fukushima-ku, Osaka|Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City]]. It was formerly classified as a [[Sonsha|village shrine]]. The shrine is also known colloquially as '''"Fukushima Lower Tenjin"''' (福島 下の天神).

== '''Enshrined Deities''' ==

[[File:Fukushima-Tenjinsya-01.jpg|160px|right|thumb]]

=== Main Deities ===

* '''[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]''' (少彦名命)
* '''{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}''' (Sugawara no Michizane)

== '''History''' ==

According to shrine tradition, in '''901''', {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} prayed for safe sea travel at this shrine when he was exiled to '''{{ill|Dazaifu|en|Dazaifu|WD=Q1179277|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu}}''' by '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|WD=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|1=Fujiwara no Tokihira}}'''. Another theory suggests that the shrine was established at the spot where a '''divine float''' (*hoko*) from the "Boat Procession" (*Funatogyo*) of the '''Tenma Tenjin Summer Festival''' drifted ashore, designating it as a temporary '''gyōkū''' (*imperial lodging*)<ref>[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/fukushima/page/0000000858.html Osaka City Fukushima Ward: Landmarks & Historical Sites]</ref>.

Sugawara no Michizane is also said to have renamed the area from '''Gakishima''' (餓飢島, meaning "Starvation Island") to '''Fukushima''' (福島, meaning "Blessed Island"). Since then, the shrine has been revered as a '''Tenjin Shrine''' (*Tenjin-sama*).

During the '''Genroku period''' (1688–1704), the current worship hall (*Haiden*) was constructed. In '''1872''' (*Meiji 5*), the shrine was classified as a village shrine (*Sonsha*). In '''1907''' (*Meiji 40*), the shrine merged with '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''.

== '''Sub-Shrines''' ==

* '''Toyomitsu Shrine'''
* '''Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine and Six Other Shrines'''
* '''Miyabito Heisha Shrine'''
* '''Itsukushima Shrine (Enoki Shrine)'''

== '''Fukushima's Three Tenjin Shrines''' ==

* '''Upper Tenjin''' (*Kami no Tenjin*) – '''Fukushima Tenmangū''', located at 2-8-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward.
* '''Middle Tenjin''' (*Naka no Tenjin*) – Formerly located at 4-1 Fukushima, Fukushima Ward (next to Shimo-Fukushima Park); merged with Fukushima Tenmangū after being destroyed in '''World War II''' air raids.
* '''Lower Tenjin''' (*Shimo no Tenjin*) – '''This shrine'''.

== '''Access''' ==

== '''Address''' ==

- 1-4-5 Tamagawa, Fukushima Ward, Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan.

=== '''Transportation''' ===

* '''Nearest Train Stations''':
** '''Keihan Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|en|Keihan Nakanoshima Line|WD=Q126331|lt=Keihan Nakanoshima Line|1=Keihan Nakanoshima Line}} '''Nakanoshima Station''' (3-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|JR Tōzai Line|en|JR Tōzai Line|WD=Q1072835|lt=JR Tōzai Line|1=JR Tōzai Line}} '''Shin-Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''JR West''' – {{ill|Osaka Loop Line|en|Osaka Loop Line|WD=Q1142127|lt=Osaka Loop Line|1=Osaka Loop Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (10-minute walk)
** '''Hanshin Electric Railway''' – {{ill|Hanshin Main Line|en|Hanshin Main Line|WD=Q1053647|lt=Hanshin Main Line|1=Hanshin Main Line}} '''Fukushima Station''' (15-minute walk)
** '''Osaka Metro Chūō Line''' – '''Tamagawa Station''' (7-minute walk)

* '''Nearest Bus Stops''':
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''55 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Kita-zume Stop''' (2-minute walk)
** '''Osaka City Bus''' '''53 Route''' – '''Dōjima Ōhashi Stop''' (3-minute walk)

 | Deities = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
 | type = Village Shrine (*Sonsha*)
